title: Elaphoglossum viride
intro: Elaphoglossum viride, also known as the green Elaphoglossum, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, E. viride is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its purported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of E. viride include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, E. viride is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum viride is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Elaphoglossum, which consists of over 1,000 species of terrestrial ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns or buckler ferns. Elaphoglossum viride is native to Central and South America. This species of fern is known for its small size and delicate fronds.
